description: Single sign AB 67 incised after firing under base of conical cup (Rethymnon Mus. inv. P 36184); MM III-LM I context; found 2014 (Tzigounaki)
publication: Tzigounaki & Karnava 2020, in Stampolidis & Giannopoulou (eds) I Eleftherna i Kriti kai o Exo Kosmos (Rethymno conf. 2018)
notes: Reading AB 67 printed in rapport; parallels KE Zb 3 and MI Zb 1 cited; ed.pr. in Greek print proceedings

description: Four AB 131a signs on one pithos, Western Magazine IX: GORILA IV KN Zb <36>/<37> renumbered 36a/36b (Firth), plus 36c (Boskamp 1991-92) and 36d (Christakis)
publication: Boskamp 1996 ABSA 91, 101-112; Christakis 2005, 60; Firth 2016 Minos 39, 229-247; rapport pp. 98-99
notes: AMBIGUITY: script attribution uncertain — A 131a and Linear B VIN homomorphic; Brice dates pithos LM I, Christakis LM IIIA (could be Linear B); KN Zb <38> identification with Boskamp's third sign uncertain

description: Ivory ring, quadrangular section, diam. ~13.7 cm; LA syllabic sequences + logograms incised on four sides; found 2016 (Kanta), neopalatial cult building Room 1 (Herakleion Ephorate D 3460/D 3458)
publication: INEDIT at rapport date; ed.pr. by Kanta, Nakassis, Palaima & Perna in preparation; overview: Kanta et al., KO-RO-NO-WE-SA (Ariadne Suppl. 5) 27-43; Kanta 2019, 250-252 cat. 280
notes: Rapport describes but prints NO reading; the Kanta et al. overview article (same OA volume, already in corpus/bronze/kanta_etal_2024_anetaki) is the interim source; check whether ed.pr. has since appeared

description: Ivory bar, quadrangular section, 13.2 cm; LA signs incised on four sides + one end; same 2016 votive deposit as KN Zg 57 (possible sceptre handle)
publication: INEDIT at rapport date; ed.pr. by Kanta, Nakassis, Palaima & Perna in preparation; overview: Kanta et al., KO-RO-NO-WE-SA (Ariadne Suppl. 5) 27-43; Kanta 2019, 250-253
notes: Rapport prints NO reading; same interim source as KN Zg 57

description: Inscribed vase fragment found by a tourist on Kommos parking lot summer 2019, handed to Herakleion Ephorate; 3 lines between guide lines, line 2 = sequence of 3 signs
publication: Unpublished; reported to Del Freo by A. Greco (pers. comm.)
notes: AMBIGUITY: 'pourrait être en linéaire A' — script attribution unconfirmed; no designation assigned; no reading printed

description: Roundel; first sign on recto proposed by Hallager as new sign A 373; Del Freo & Zurbach: variant of the tripod logogram
publication: Andreadaki-Vlazaki & Hallager 2007, Proc. Danish Inst. Athens 5, 7-22 (13, Fig. 5); discussed in Comite des signes resolutions, same KO-RO-NO-WE-SA volume
notes: Sign-identity dispute only, not a new inscription; item predates reporting period (cf. Rapport 2006-2010, 8)

description: Roundel (ME 117) found 2007, 15 impressions of seal KH 13 on rim; recto sign = variant of AB 61 (not AB 54 as previously reported); LM IB by seal-link dating
publication: Andreadaki-Vlazaki & Hallager 2018, Kadmos 57, 33-44 (37-39, Fig. 4)
notes: Corrected sign-reading printed in rapport; ed.pr. Kadmos 57 is paywalled (De Gruyter); footnote 76: {KH Wc 1025} to be renumbered {KH Wc 2125}, possible anepigraphic roundel {Wc 2126}

description: Tablet fragment (GSDE 10-TC 011), 2010 excavation, LM IIIB1 destruction layer (mixed); recto AB 08-73[ or AB 08-65[
publication: Andreadaki-Vlazaki & Hallager 2018, Kadmos 57, 33-44 (33-34, Fig. 1)
notes: Reading printed in rapport (both alternatives); ed.pr. Kadmos 57 paywalled

description: Tablet fragment (GSDE 14-TC 008), 2014 excavation, Geometric deposit with sporadic LM IB; fractional quantities (A 707) of grain (AB 120) after syllabic sequences incl. AB 01-27
publication: Andreadaki-Vlazaki & Hallager 2018, Kadmos 57, 33-44 (34-36, Fig. 2)
notes: Partial reading printed; rapport (Del Freo & Zurbach autopsy) REJECTS Hallager's proposed new ligature AB 120+26 on line .2; ed.pr. Kadmos 57 paywalled

description: Small tablet fragment (GSDE 14-TC 038), 2014 excavation, sealed LM IIIB2 deposit; lower-left corner; AB 45-67-37[
publication: Andreadaki-Vlazaki & Hallager 2018, Kadmos 57, 33-44 (36-37, Fig. 3)
notes: Reading (editors' proposal, partly fragmentary) printed in rapport; ed.pr. Kadmos 57 paywalled

description: Fragmentary miniature painted cup (74-P 0206), found 1974, LM IIIA1 context (7/8-Pit A), probably Knossian; possible painted LA inscription in two registers: AB 73-60-61-[ (upper), AB 55-[.]-70-55-[ (lower, uncertain)

notes: AMBIGUITY: LA attribution itself uncertain (rapport urges prudence); alternatives AB 37/AB 17 for two signs; possibly sinistroverse (parallel PO Zg 1); ed.pr. in print monograph (Swedish Institute)

description: Re-reading proposal for known inscribed handle: Hopkins AB 73-01-51 (hapax) vs ed.pr. Schoep & Driessen AB 73-01-60 (hapax); rapport finds new reading no better
publication: Hopkins 2018, Kadmos 57, 15-20 (18-20); ed.pr. Schoep & Driessen 2002-2003, Minos 37-38, 77-80
notes: Reading dispute only, item already in corpus; both alternatives printed in rapport; Kadmos paywalled, Minos 37-38 older OA-ish journal

description: Fragmentary inscribed libation table found near the peak sanctuary (announced in Rapport 2011-2015)
publication: Knappett, Del Freo & Zurbach 2017, SMEA NS 3, 73-82, Fig. 1
notes: Rapport only records publication, prints no reading here; SMEA NS 3 (Quasar) not OA — transliteration lives in the ed.pr.; GAP unless SMEA article acquired

description: Fragment of inscribed libation table, surface find June 2016 near peak sanctuary; 13th of its type from Petsophas; ]-AB 29-27 | .[ — probably final part of the libation-formula sequence as on PK Za 8a / PK Za 15
publication: Del Freo, Zurbach & Knappett 2022, in Driessen & Knappett (eds) Megistos Kouros (Aegis 23), 128-131
notes: Reading printed in rapport; Aegis series (PUL) is generally OA PDF — unverified for this volume; sign after divider fragmentary

description: Tablet fragment previously PE <6> (lost/unlocated), relocated 2017 in Siteia Museum storerooms by Del Freo & Zurbach; reclassified PE <6> -> PE 6
publication: Status note in rapport (cf. Rapport 2011-2015, 13); GORILA for the text
notes: Bookkeeping change only (brackets removed); no new reading printed; affects designation normalization in inventory

description: Vase fragment (inv. SAIA F8308) found 2017, Italian mission excavation; two 2-sign sequences incised after firing (wall + lip); vase form suggests protopalatial; signs possibly LA, difficult
publication: Unpublished; publication assigned to A. Greco
notes: AMBIGUITY: 'seraient rapportables au lineaire A' per Greco; no designation, no reading printed; check for Greco publication after 2021

description: Vase fragment with signs incised after firing, found 2017 in stratigraphic sounding, archaic pit (ceramic date LM III, Greco prefers neopalatial by the inscription); Greco reads AB 39-58 A301+A303 (alt. AB 21f+A303); Godart: AB 21f 20 (or 30) = ostracon with commodity annotation
publication: Greco 2022, Pasiphae 16, 153-166
notes: Candidate readings printed in rapport with detailed doubts (AB 39 shape anomalous; proposed ligatures unattested); ed.pr. Pasiphae 16 (Serra) paywalled; no GORILA-style designation given

description: Stone stepped-pyramid libation table, found 2013 (Sapouna-Sakellaraki), Central Building Room 53, MM III-LM I; 14 incised signs, some resembling AB 04/77/02/44/28/86
publication: Sapouna-Sakellaraki 2015 (Prakt), 282-283, Fig. 15, Pl. 167; cf. Rapport 2011-2015, 15
notes: NEGATIVE VERDICT: rapport judges an LA inscription 'tres improbable' — signs likely masons' marks (matches Hood 2020 types); candidate sign IDs printed; EXCLUDE from LA corpus unless overturned

description: Graffito on lamp (EfA inv. V 15); findspot between Xeste 2 and Complex Delta or Sector Beta; possible reading AB 09-A332 2 (doubts persist)
publication: Tzachili & Karnava 2016, Minos 39, 27-40
notes: NUMBERING AMBIGUITY: editors number it Zb 15, rapport argues for Zb 14 (per Rapport 2001-2005, 210 vs Rapport 2006-2010); normalize carefully; Minos is OA on journal site (unverified)

description: Discoid clay loomweight, signs incised before firing, found 2001 (Pillar Pit 66P), Middle Cycladic = early MM III context; imported (probably Cretan); reading AB 164a-53-01 (hapax)
publication: Karnava 2019, in Nikolakopoulou (ed.) Akrotiri Thera MBA pottery vol. I (Arch. Society Athens Library 318), 501-504
notes: Reading printed in rapport (dotted/uncertain); possibly oldest LA inscription from Thera and first LA on a clay loomweight; classification queried (?); ed.pr. print monograph

description: Fragment of faience triton with PAINTED inscription, found 2019 (Doumas), Room 1 House of the Benches (cult context, south of Xeste 3); legible from photo: ]AB 78 | AB 28-67-26-78 (alt. AB 28-81-...)
publication: INEDIT at rapport date; publication assigned to A. Karnava; context: Doumas 2020 Prakt 2019, 287-300, Fig. 9
notes: Provisional reading printed in rapport (second sign uncertain, sequence hapax either way); check for Karnava ed.pr. after 2021

description: Fragmentary cup base (inv. P 24906), one or two signs incised (perhaps before firing), Davaras excavations 1972-73, no stratigraphic date; ]AB 34-31[ or ]AB 11-31[ (earlier Rapport read ]AB 73[)
publication: Tzachili & Georgakopoulos 2015, in Arkhaiologiko Ergo Kritis 3 (Rethymno 2013) vol. 2, 289-298 (294-295, Fig. 8)
notes: Provisional classification with (?); preliminary publication only; reading alternatives printed in rapport

description: Kyathos fragment (inv. P 24605), two incised signs; ]AB 37-04[ or ]AB 10-04[ (AB 10 sinistroverse); Cretan Hieroglyphic ]093-025[ not excluded
publication: Tzachili & Georgakopoulos 2015, ibid. (293-294, Fig. 6)
notes: AMBIGUITY: script attribution LA vs Hieroglyphic open; first sign fragmentary, other readings possible; provisional classification assigned by rapport

description: Fragment of stone libation table (inv. L 4098), one sign + possible traces of second; AB 10-.[ — surviving sign without exact parallel, vague resemblance to AB 10 variants on Iouktas tables (IO Za 2b.2, Za 6)
publication: Tzachili & Georgakopoulos 2015, ibid. (295, Fig. 9)
notes: Provisional classification assigned by rapport; reading printed with heavy uncertainty

description: New join to tablet ZA 11 from fragments found among 1960s excavation sherds in Siteia Museum

description: New join to tablet ZA 13 from fragments found among 1960s excavation sherds in Siteia Museum
publication: To be published by V. Petrakis within L. Platon's publication of palace Room XVI
notes: Join announced only; no reading printed

description: Vase fragment (MSKN MP/71/79), two signs incised before firing, found 1971, LM I context with possible MM cup fragment; REATTRIBUTED from LA (PYR Zb 5) to Cretan Hieroglyphic PYR Yb 01 (?): readings ]025-056[ / ]025-019[ / ]027-056[ / ]027-019[ vs old ]AB 04-57[
publication: Ferrara, Weingarten & Cadogan 2016, SMEA NS 2, 81-99 (87-88, 94-95, Fig. 7); cf. Rapport 1991-1995, 428-429
notes: REMOVAL candidate from LA corpus: rapport endorses hieroglyphic attribution ('ne semble pas inappropriee'); Olivier's original LA attribution was 'sans trop de conviction'; normalize PYRZb5 accordingly

description: Four-sided glass-paste/steatite seal (Herakleion Ephorate inv. D 3702), found 2016 (Kanta); Archanes formula 042-019 | 019-095-052 on face alpha; classified HIEROGLYPHIC in rapport but LA attribution argued (Godart 1999; Anastasiadou 2016: cf. AB 08-31-31-60-13 on libation tables)
publication: Kanta, Palaima & Perna 2022, Kadmos 61, 61-96
notes: AMBIGUITY row (listed under Hieroglyphic in rapport): Archanes-formula script attribution CH vs LA 'reste problematique'; 3-4 face-gamma signs correspond better to LA; ed.pr. Kadmos 61 paywalled; include only if inventory counts Archanes-formula items as LA

description: Nodulus; footnote observation: presence of klasmatogram A 708 (exclusively LA) on SA We 3 suggests We 4 also bears an LA inscription (AB 08+04 per Olivier, ligature vs 'decorative motif' debate)
publication: Rapport 2016-2021 fn. 14-15, citing Rapport 2001-2005, 208-209
notes: Footnote-level attribution note only; item already known; affects LA/CH classification of Samothrace noduli

description: PREDECESSOR RAPPORT NOT ACQUIRED — coverage hole for LA finds/editions 2011-2015 (only items resolved later are recapped in Rapport 2016-2021, e.g. PK Za 27, PE <6>, Zominthos, KH 102/104/105 announcements)
